paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The pyramids of Egypt were built more than three thousand years ago, and no one knows how. The conventional picture is that tens of thousands of slaves dragged stones on sledges. But there is no evidence to back this up. Now a Californian software consultant called Maureen Clemmons has suggested that kites might have been involved. While perusing a book on the monuments of Egypt, she noticed a hieroglyph that showed a row of men standing in odd postures. They were holding what looked like ropes that led, via some kind of mechanical system, to a giant bird in the sky. She wondered if perhaps the bird was actually a giant kite, and the men were using it to lift a heavy object.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Intrigued, Clemmons contacted Morteza Gharib, aeronautics professor at the California Institute of Technology. He was fascinated by the idea. 'Coming from Iran, I have a keen interest in Middle Eastern science,' he says. He too was puzzled by the picture that had sparked Clemmons's interest. The object in the sky apparently had wings far too short and wide for a bird. 'The possibility certainly existed that it was a kite,' he says. And since he needed a summer project for his student Emilio Graff, investigating the possibility of using kites as heavy lifters seemed like a good idea.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Gharib and Graff set themselves the task of raising a 4.5-metre stone column from horizontal to vertical, using no source of energy except the wind. Their initial calculations and scale-model wind-tunnel experiments convinced them they wouldn't need a strong wind to lift the 33.5-tonne column. Even a modest force, if sustained over a long time, would do. The key was to use a pulley system that would magnify the applied force. So they rigged up a tent-shaped scaffold directly above the tip of the horizontal column, with pulleys suspended from the scaffold's apex. The idea was that as one end of the column rose, the base would roll across the ground on a trolley. Earlier this year, the team put Clemmons's unlikely theory to the test, using a 40-square-metre rectangular nylon sail. The kite lifted the column clean off the ground. 'We were absolutely stunned,' Gharib says. 'The instant the sail opened into the wind, a huge force was generated and the column was raised to the vertical in a mere 40 seconds.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The wind was blowing at a gentle 16 to 20 kilometres an hour, little more than half what they thought would be needed. What they had failed to reckon with was what happened when the kite was opened. 'There was a huge initial force—five times larger than the steady state force,' Gharib says. This jerk meant that kites could lift huge weights, Gharib realised. Even a 300-tonne column could have been lifted to the vertical with 40 or so men and four or five sails. So Clemmons was right: the pyramid builders could have used kites to lift massive stones into place. 'Whether they actually did is another matter,' Gharib says. There are no pictures showing the construction of the pyramids, so there is no way to tell what really happened. 'The evidence for using kites to move large stones is no better or worse than the evidence for the brute force method,' Gharib says.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Indeed, the experiments have left many specialists unconvinced. 'The evidence for kite-lifting is non-existent,' says Willeke Wendrich, an associate professor of Egyptology at the University of California, Los Angeles.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Others feel there is more of a case for the theory. Harnessing the wind would not have been a problem for accomplished sailors like the Egyptians. And they are known to have used wooden pulleys, which could have been made strong enough to bear the weight of massive blocks of stone. In addition, there is some physical evidence that the ancient Egyptians were interested in flight. A wooden artefact found on the step pyramid at Saqqara looks uncannily like a modern glider. Although it dates from several hundred years after the building of the pyramids, its sophistication suggests that the Egyptians might have been developing ideas of flight for a long time. And other ancient civilisations certainly knew about kites; as early as 1250 BC, the Chinese were using them to deliver messages and dump flaming debris on their foes.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The experiments might even have practical uses nowadays. There are plenty of places around the globe where people have no access to heavy machinery, but do know how to deal with wind, sailing and basic mechanical principles. Gharib has already been contacted by a civil engineer in Nicaragua, who wants to put up buildings with adobe roofs supported by concrete arches on a site that heavy equipment can't reach. His idea is to build the arches horizontally, then lift them into place using kites. 'We've given him some design hints,' says Gharib. 'We're just waiting for him to report back.' So whether they were actually used to build the pyramids or not, it seems that kites may make sensible construction tools in the 21st century AD.

questions.title: It is generally believed that large numbers of people were needed to build the pyramids.

content.title: Endless Harvest
content.notes: ['* spawners: fish that have released eggs', '** spawn: release eggs', '*** MSC: a joint venture between WWF (World Wildlife Fund) and Unilever, a Dutch-based multi-national']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: More than two hundred years ago, Russian explorers and fur hunters landed on the Aleutian Islands, a volcanic archipelago in the North Pacific, and learned of a land mass that lay farther to the north. The islands' native inhabitants called this land mass Aleyska, the 'Great Land'; today, we know it as Alaska.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The forty-ninth state to join the United States of America (in 1959), Alaska is fully one-fifth the size of the mainland 48 states combined. It shares, with Canada, the second longest river system in North America and has over half the coastline of the United States. The rivers feed into the Bering Sea and Gulf of Alaska - cold, nutrient-rich waters which support tens of millions of seabirds, and over 400 species of fish, shellfish, crustaceans, and molluscs. Taking advantage of this rich bounty, Alaska's commercial fisheries have developed into some of the largest in the world.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: According to the Alaska Department of Fish and Game (ADF&G), Alaska's commercial fisheries landed hundreds of thousands of tonnes of shellfish and herring, and well over a million tonnes of groundfish (cod, sole, perch and pollock) in 2000. The true cultural heart and soul of Alaska's fisheries, however, is salmon. 'Salmon,' notes writer Susan Ewing in The Great Alaska Nature Factbook, 'pump through Alaska like blood through a heart, bringing rhythmic, circulating nourishment to land, animals and people.' The 'predictable abundance of salmon allowed some native cultures to flourish,' and 'dying spawners* feed bears, eagles, other animals, and ultimately the soil itself.' All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there. Indeed, if Alaska was an independent nation, it would be the largest producer of wild salmon in the world. During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Catches have not always been so healthy. Between 1940 and 1959, overfishing led to crashes in salmon populations so severe that in 1953 Alaska was declared a federal disaster area. With the onset of statehood, however, the State of Alaska took over management of its own fisheries, guided by a state constitution which mandates that Alaska's natural resources be managed on a sustainable basis. At that time, statewide harvests totalled around 25 million salmon. Over the next few decades average catches steadily increased as a result of this policy of sustainable management, until, during the 1990s, annual harvests were well in excess of 100 million, and on several occasions over 200 million fish.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The primary reason for such increases is what is known as 'In-Season Abundance-Based Management'. There are biologists throughout the state constantly monitoring adult fish as they show up to spawn. The biologists sit in streamside counting towers, study sonar, watch from aeroplanes, and talk to fishermen. The salmon season in Alaska is not pre-set. The fishermen know the approximate time of year when they will be allowed to fish, but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing. Even sport fishing can be brought to a halt. It is this management mechanism that has allowed Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries - to prosper, even as salmon populations in the rest of the United States are increasingly considered threatened or even endangered.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In 1999, the Marine Stewardship Council (MSC)*** commissioned a review of the Alaska salmon fishery. The Council, which was founded in 1996, certifies fisheries that meet high environmental standards, enabling them to use a label that recognises their environmental responsibility. The MSC has established a set of criteria by which commercial fisheries can be judged. Recognising the potential benefits of being identified as environmentally responsible, fisheries approach the Council requesting to undergo the certification process. The MSC then appoints a certification committee, composed of a panel of fisheries experts, which gathers information and opinions from fishermen, biologists, government officials, industry representatives, non-governmental organisations and others.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Some observers thought the Alaska salmon fisheries would not have any chance of certification when, in the months leading up to MSC's final decision, salmon runs throughout western Alaska completely collapsed. In the Yukon and Kuskokwim rivers, chinook and chum runs were probably the poorest since statehood; subsistence communities throughout the region, who normally have priority over commercial fishing, were devastated.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The crisis was completely unexpected, but researchers believe it had nothing to do with impacts of fisheries. Rather, they contend, it was almost certainly the result of climatic shifts, prompted in part by cumulative effects of the el niño / la niña phenomenon on Pacific Ocean temperatures, culminating in a harsh winter in which huge numbers of salmon eggs were frozen. It could have meant the end as far as the certification process was concerned. However, the state reacted quickly, closing down all fisheries, even those necessary for subsistence purposes.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: In September 2000, MSC announced that the Alaska salmon fisheries qualified for certification. Seven companies producing Alaska salmon were immediately granted permission to display the MSC logo on their products. Certification is for an initial period of five years, with an annual review to ensure that the fishery is continuing to meet the required standards.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-20

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 2?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The inhabitants of the Aleutian islands renamed their islands 'Aleyska'.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息与文章中的描述不一致。题干说的是阿留申群岛的居民将他们的岛屿重新命名为 "Aleyska"，而文章中说的是他们称呼一块陆地为 "Aleyska"。因此，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The islands' native inhabitants called this land mass Aleyska, the 'Great Land'; today, we know it as Alaska.
central_sentences.translation: 这些岛屿的原住民称这片陆地为Aleyska，意为"大地"；如今，我们称之为阿拉斯加。

target_sen.target_sen: The islands' native inhabitants called this land mass Aleyska, the 'Great Land'; today, we know it as Alaska.

questions.title: Alaska's fisheries are owned by some of the world's largest companies.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“Alaska's fisheries”、“owned by”和“some of the world's largest companies”在原文中没有找到对应的同义替换词或相关信息。文章主要讨论了阿拉斯加渔业的规模、管理和环境认证等内容，但没有提到这些渔业的所有权归属。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提供关于阿拉斯加渔业所有权的信息。

central_sentences.analysis: 文中谈规模与管理权归州政府，但未说明“由世界最大公司拥有”。
central_sentences.keywords: commercial fisheries, largest, took over management
sentences: Taking advantage of this rich bounty, Alaska's commercial fisheries have developed into some of the largest in the world.

With the onset of statehood, however, the State of Alaska took over management of its own fisheries, guided by a state constitution which mandates that Alaska's natural resources be managed on a sustainable basis.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: owned by companies -> 无对应信息; fisheries -> commercial fisheries

questions.title: Life in Alaska is dependent on salmon.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到鲑鱼对阿拉斯加的土地、动物和人类的重要性，表明了鲑鱼对阿拉斯加生活的依赖性。题干中的“Life in Alaska is dependent on salmon”与文章中的“Salmon pump through Alaska like blood through a heart, bringing rhythmic, circulating nourishment to land, animals and people”直接对应，说明了鲑鱼对阿拉斯加生活的关键作用。因此，题干信息在文章中是可以找到的。

central_sentences.sentence: pump through Alaska like blood through a heart, bringing rhythmic, circulating nourishment to land, animals and people.' The
central_sentences.translation: 鲑鱼像血液流经心脏一样流经阿拉斯加，为土地、动物和人类带来有规律的、循环的滋养。

central_sentences.sentence: The 'predictable abundance of salmon allowed some native cultures to flourish
central_sentences.translation: 可预测的丰富鲑鱼资源使一些原住民文化得以繁荣。

questions.title: Ninety per cent of all Pacific salmon caught are sockeye or pink salmon.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“90%的太平洋鲑鱼是sockeye或pink salmon”在文章中没有具体提到，也没有任何信息可以直接或间接推导出这点。文章中提到的90%只是指阿拉斯加捕获的太平洋鲑鱼总量，并未具体说明sockeye和pink在这90%中的比例。因此，答案是NOT GIVEN。学生应注意文章中是否有明确的信息支持或反驳题干中的陈述，而不是仅凭相似的数字或关键词进行判断。

central_sentences.sentence: All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there.
central_sentences.translation: 所有五种太平洋鲑鱼——大鲑鱼（或王鲑）、大麻哈鱼（或狗鲑）、银鲑、红鲑（或红大麻哈鱼）和粉鲑（或驼背鲑）——都在阿拉斯加水域产卵，北美商业捕捞的太平洋鲑鱼中90%都产自那里。

target_sen.target_sen: All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there.

All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there.
During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.

All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there.

All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there.

questions.title: More than 320,000 tonnes of salmon were caught in Alaska in 2000.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "More than 320,000 tonnes of salmon were caught in Alaska in 2000" 与原文中的 "During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes" 完全一致。题干中的 "More than" 对应原文中的 "exceeded"，"320,000 tonnes" 和 "salmon" 以及 "2000" 在原文中也有直接对应。因此，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.
central_sentences.translation: 2000年，阿拉斯加的太平洋鲑鱼商业捕获量超过32万吨，船上价值超过2.6亿美元。

target_sen.target_sen: During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.
During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.

According to the Alaska Department of Fish and Game (ADF&G), Alaska's commercial fisheries landed hundreds of thousands of tonnes of shellfish and herring, and well over a million tonnes of groundfish (cod, sole, perch and pollock) in 2000.
During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.

According to the Alaska Department of Fish and Game (ADF&G), Alaska's commercial fisheries landed hundreds of thousands of tonnes of shellfish and herring, and well over a million tonnes of groundfish (cod, sole, perch and pollock) in 2000.
The true cultural heart and soul of Alaska's fisheries, however, is salmon.
'Salmon,' notes writer Susan Ewing in The Great Alaska Nature Factbook, 'pump through Alaska like blood through a heart, bringing rhythmic, circulating nourishment to land, animals and people.'

All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there.
Indeed, if Alaska was an independent nation, it would be the largest producer of wild salmon in the world.
During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.

questions.title: Between 1940 and 1959, there was a sharp decrease in Alaska's salmon population.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到在1940到1959年间，由于过度捕捞，阿拉斯加的鲑鱼数量急剧下降，甚至在1953年被宣布为联邦灾区。这与题干中的描述完全一致。通过识别时间段和描述数量变化的词汇，学生可以准确地判断出答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Between 1940 and 1959, overfishing led to crashes in salmon populations so severe that in 1953 Alaska was declared a federal disaster area.
central_sentences.translation: 在1940年到1959年间，过度捕捞导致鲑鱼种群数量急剧下降，情况如此严重，以至于1953年阿拉斯加被宣布为联邦灾区。

target_sen.target_sen: Between 1940 and 1959, overfishing led to crashes in salmon populations so severe that in 1953 Alaska was declared a federal disaster area.
Between 1940 and 1959, overfishing led to crashes in salmon populations so severe that in 1953 Alaska was declared a federal disaster area.

questions.title: During the 1990s, the average number of salmon caught each year was 100 million.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“1990年代，每年捕捞的鲑鱼平均数为1亿”，而原文中明确提到“1990年代，年捕捞量远远超过1亿，并且在几次情况下超过2亿”。这表明题干中的信息与原文内容不符，因此答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Over the next few decades average catches steadily increased as a result of this policy of sustainable management, until, during the 1990s, annual harvests were well in excess of 100 million, and on several occasions over 200 million fish.
central_sentences.translation: 在接下来的几十年里，由于这种可持续管理政策，平均捕获量稳步增加，到了1990年代，年捕获量远超过1亿条，并且在几次情况下超过2亿条鱼。

target_sen.target_sen: Over the next few decades average catches steadily increased as a result of this policy of sustainable management, until, during the 1990s, annual harvests were well in excess of 100 million, and on several occasions over 200 million fish.

Over the next few decades average catches steadily increased as a result of this policy of sustainable management, until, during the 1990s, annual harvests were well in excess of 100 million, and on several occasions over 200 million fish.

Over the next few decades average catches steadily increased as a result of this policy of sustainable management, until, during the 1990s, annual harvests were well in excess of 100 million, and on several occasions over 200 million fish.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: to recognise fisheries that care for the environment.
to set up environmental protection laws.
to stop people fishing for sport.
to label their products using the MSC logo.
to ensure that fish numbers are sufficient to permit fishing.
to assist the subsistence communities in the region.
to freeze a huge number of salmon eggs.
to deny certification to the Alaska fisheries.

questions.title: In Alaska, biologists keep a check on adult fish
questions.analysis: 文章中提到，生物学家监控成鱼的目的是为了决定是否允许捕鱼。这与选项 G "to ensure that fish numbers are sufficient to permit fishing" 完全一致。因此，正确答案是 G。

central_sentences.sentence: There are biologists throughout the state constantly monitoring adult fish as they show up to spawn. The biologists sit in streamside counting towers, study sonar, watch from aeroplanes, and talk to fishermen. The salmon season in Alaska is not pre-set. The fishermen know the approximate time of year when they will be allowed to fish, but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing.
central_sentences.translation: 全州各地的生物学家不断监测成年鱼类的产卵情况。生物学家坐在溪流旁的计数塔中，研究声纳，从飞机上观察，并与渔民交谈。阿拉斯加的鲑鱼季节并非预先设定。渔民知道大致允许捕鱼的时间，但在任何给定的日子里，某个地区的一个或多个实地生物学家都可以叫停捕鱼活动。

target_sen.target_sen: There are biologists throughout the state constantly monitoring adult fish as they show up to spawn.
The biologists sit in streamside counting towers, study sonar, watch from aeroplanes, and talk to fishermen.
The fishermen know the approximate time of year when they will be allowed to fish, but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing.
There are biologists throughout the state constantly monitoring adult fish as they show up to spawn.
There are biologists throughout the state constantly monitoring adult fish as they show up to spawn.

questions.title: Biologists have the authority
questions.analysis: 题干问的是生物学家有权力做什么。通过文章中的句子“one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing. Even sport fishing can be brought to a halt.”，我们可以看到生物学家有权力停止捕鱼活动，甚至包括休闲捕鱼。因此，正确答案是E: to stop people fishing for sport。

central_sentences.sentence: Even sport fishing can be brought to a halt.
central_sentences.translation: 即使是休闲钓鱼活动也可以被叫停。

target_sen.target_sen: The fishermen know the approximate time of year when they will be allowed to fish, but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing.
The fishermen know the approximate time of year when they will be allowed to fish, but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing.

questions.title: In-Season Abundance-Based Management has allowed the Alaska salmon fisheries
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "In-Season Abundance-Based Management" 和 "allowed" 在原文中对应 "this management mechanism" 和 "has allowed"。原文中提到这种管理机制使得阿拉斯加的鲑鱼种群和渔业得以繁荣（prosper），这与题干中的 "successful" 是同义表达。因此，正确答案是 B: to be successful。

central_sentences.sentence: It is this management mechanism that has allowed Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries - to prosper, even as salmon populations in the rest of the United States are increasingly considered threatened or even endangered.
central_sentences.translation: 正是这种管理机制使得阿拉斯加鲑鱼种群——相应地，阿拉斯加鲑鱼渔业得以繁荣，即使美国其他地区的鲑鱼种群越来越多地被认为受到威胁甚至濒临灭绝。

target_sen.target_sen: It is this management mechanism that has allowed Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries - to prosper, even as salmon populations in the rest of the United States are increasingly considered threatened or even endangered.
The fishermen know the approximate time of year when they will be allowed to fish, but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing.

It is this management mechanism that has allowed Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries - to prosper, even as salmon populations in the rest of the United States are increasingly considered threatened or even endangered.

It is this management mechanism that has allowed Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries - to prosper, even as salmon populations in the rest of the United States are increasingly considered threatened or even endangered.

questions.title: The Marine Stewardship Council (MSC) was established
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A: to recognise fisheries that care for the environment。题干中的“established”在原文中对应的同义词是“founded”，“recognise”对应的是“certifies”和“recognises”，“environment”对应的是“environmental”。原文中明确提到MSC的目的是认证符合高环境标准的渔业，并认可它们的环境责任，这与选项A的意思完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: The Council, which was founded in 1996, certifies fisheries that meet high environmental standards, enabling them to use a label that recognises their environmental responsibility.
central_sentences.translation: 该委员会成立于1996年，负责认证符合高环境标准的渔业，使它们能够使用一个标签来认可其环境责任。

target_sen.target_sen: In 1999, the Marine Stewardship Council (MSC)*** commissioned a review of the Alaska salmon fishery.
The Council, which was founded in 1996, certifies fisheries that meet high environmental standards, enabling them to use a label that recognises their environmental responsibility.

questions.title: As a result of the collapse of the salmon runs in 1999, the state decided
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "collapse" 对应文章中的 "collapsed"，"the state decided" 对应文章中的 "the state reacted quickly"。文章明确提到 "closing down all fisheries"，这与选项 K 完全一致。因此，正确答案是 K。

central_sentences.sentence: However, the state reacted quickly, closing down all fisheries, even those necessary for subsistence purposes.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，州政府迅速做出反应，关闭了所有渔业，甚至包括那些维持生计所必需的渔业。

target_sen.target_sen: However, the state reacted quickly, closing down all fisheries, even those necessary for subsistence purposes.

questions.title: In September 2000, the MSC allowed seven Alaska salmon companies
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "In September 2000" 和 "seven Alaska salmon companies" 是关键线索词，文章中明确提到 "In September 2000, MSC announced that the Alaska salmon fisheries qualified for certification. Seven companies producing Alaska salmon were immediately granted permission to display the MSC logo on their products." 这句话与题干高度对应，说明七家公司获得了使用 MSC 标志的许可。因此，正确答案是 F: to label their products using the MSC logo。

central_sentences.sentence: In September 2000, MSC announced that the Alaska salmon fisheries qualified for certification. Seven companies producing Alaska salmon were immediately granted permission to display the MSC logo on their products.
central_sentences.translation: 2000年9月，海洋管理委员会（MSC）宣布阿拉斯加鲑鱼渔业获得认证资格。七家生产阿拉斯加鲑鱼的公司立即获准在其产品上展示MSC标志。

target_sen.target_sen: In September 2000, MSC announced that the Alaska salmon fisheries qualified for certification.
Seven companies producing Alaska salmon were immediately granted permission to display the MSC logo on their products.
Seven companies producing Alaska salmon were immediately granted permission to display the MSC logo on their products.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In general, it is plausible to suppose that we should prefer peace and quiet to noise. And yet most of us have had the experience of having to adjust to sleeping in the mountains or the countryside because it was initially 'too quiet', an experience that suggests that humans are capable of adapting to a wide range of noise levels. Research supports this view. For example, Glass and Singer (1972) exposed people to short bursts of very loud noise and then measured their ability to work out problems and their physiological reactions to the noise. The noise was quite disruptive at first, but after about four minutes the subjects were doing just as well on their tasks as control subjects who were not exposed to noise. Their physiological arousal also declined quickly to the same levels as those of the control subjects.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: But there are limits to adaptation and loud noise becomes more troublesome if the person is required to concentrate on more than one task. For example, high noise levels interfered with the performance of subjects who were required to monitor three dials at a time, a task not unlike that of an aeroplane pilot or an air-traffic controller (Broadbent, 1957). Similarly, noise did not affect a subject's ability to track a moving line with a steering wheel, but it did interfere with the subject's ability to repeat numbers while tracking (Finkelman and Glass, 1970).

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Probably the most significant finding from research on noise is that its predictability is more important than how loud it is. We are much more able to 'tune out' chronic background noise, even if it is quite loud, than to work under circumstances with unexpected intrusions of noise. In the Glass and Singer study, in which subjects were exposed to bursts of noise as they worked on a task, some subjects heard loud bursts and others heard soft bursts. For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise). Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment. But the different noise conditions had quite different after-effects when the subjects were required to proofread written material under conditions of no noise. As shown in Table 1, the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Apparently, unpredictable noise produces more fatigue than predictable noise, but it takes a while for this fatigue to take its toll on performance.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Predictability is not the only variable that reduces or eliminates the negative effects of noise. Another is control. If the individual knows that he or she can control the noise, this seems to eliminate both its negative effects at the time and its after-effects. This is true even if the individual never actually exercises his or her option to turn the noise off (Glass and Singer, 1972). Just the knowledge that one has control is sufficient.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The studies discussed so far exposed people to noise for only short periods and only transient effects were studied. But the major worry about noisy environments is that living day after day with chronic noise may produce serious, lasting effects. One study, suggesting that this worry is a realistic one, compared elementary school pupils who attended schools near Los Angeles's busiest airport with students who attended schools in quiet neighbourhoods (Cohen et al., 1980). It was found that children from the noisy schools had higher blood pressure and were more easily distracted than those who attended the quiet schools. Moreover, there was no evidence of adaptability to the noise. In fact, the longer the children had attended the noisy schools, the more distractible they became. The effects also seem to be long lasting. A follow-up study showed that children who were moved to less noisy classrooms still showed greater distractibility one year later than students who had always been in the quiet schools (Cohen et al., 1981). It should be noted that the two groups of children had been carefully matched by the investigators so that they were comparable in age, ethnicity, race, and social class.

questions.title: The writer suggests that people may have difficulty sleeping in the mountains because

options.text: humans do not prefer peace and quiet to noise.
they may be exposed to short bursts of very strange sounds.
humans prefer to hear a certain amount of noise while they sleep.
they may have adapted to a higher noise level in the city.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到人们在山里或乡村睡觉时需要适应，因为那里“太安静”，这表明人类已经适应了城市中的较高噪音水平。因此，正确答案是D：they may have adapted to a higher noise level in the city。

central_sentences.sentence: And yet most of us have had the experience of having to adjust to sleeping in the mountains or the countryside because it was initially 'too quiet', an experience that suggests that humans are capable of adapting to a wide range of noise levels.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，我们大多数人都有过这样的经历：必须适应在山区或乡村睡觉，因为刚开始觉得"太安静了"，这种经历表明人类能够适应广泛的噪音水平。

syn_ary.synonym: adjust to sleeping in the mountains

target_sen.target_sen: And yet most of us have had the experience of having to adjust to sleeping in the mountains or the countryside because it was initially 'too quiet', an experience that suggests that humans are capable of adapting to a wide range of noise levels.

questions.title: In noise experiments, Glass and Singer found that

options.text: problem-solving is much easier under quiet conditions.
physiological arousal prevents the ability to work.
bursts of noise do not seriously disrupt problem-solving in the long term.
the physiological arousal of control subjects declined quickly.

questions.analysis: Glass 和 Singer 的实验发现，尽管噪音在开始时会干扰受试者的表现，但在短时间内（约四分钟后），受试者的表现恢复到与控制组相同的水平。这表明噪音爆发不会在长期内严重干扰问题解决能力。

central_sentences.sentence: The noise was quite disruptive at first, but after about four minutes the subjects were doing just as well on their tasks as control subjects who were not exposed to noise.
central_sentences.translation: 噪音一开始确实造成了很大的干扰，但大约四分钟后，受试者在任务中的表现和没有暴露于噪音的控制组一样好。

questions.title: Researchers discovered that high noise levels are not likely to interfere with the

options.text: successful performance of a single task.
tasks of pilots or air traffic controllers.
ability to repeat numbers while tracking moving lines.
ability to monitor three dials at once.

questions.analysis: 文章明确指出，噪音不会影响受试者用方向盘跟踪移动线条的能力，这是一项单一任务。因此，正确答案是A，即“单一任务的成功完成”。

central_sentences.sentence: Similarly, noise did not affect a subject's ability to track a moving line with a steering wheel, but it did interfere with the subject's ability to repeat numbers while tracking (Finkelman and Glass, 1970).
central_sentences.translation: 同样，噪音不会影响受试者用方向盘跟踪移动线条的能力，但会干扰受试者在跟踪过程中重复数字的能力（Finkelman和Glass，1970）。

target_sen.target_sen: But there are limits to adaptation and loud noise becomes more troublesome if the person is required to concentrate on more than one task.

Similarly, noise did not affect a subject's ability to track a moving line with a steering wheel, but it did interfere with the subject's ability to repeat numbers while tracking (Finkelman and Glass, 1970).

questions.analysis: 题干中提到“intense noise”和“less effect on performance”，在文章中对应的是“very loud noise”和“quite disruptive at first, but after about four minutes the subjects were doing just as well on their tasks”。题干中的“circumstances in which 【 】 noise occurs”在文章中对应的是“unexpected intrusions of noise”。因此，正确答案是“unexpected”。

central_sentences.sentence: Probably the most significant finding from research on noise is that its predictability is more important than how loud it is. We are much more able to 'tune out' chronic background noise, even if it is quite loud, than to work under circumstances with unexpected intrusions of noise.
central_sentences.translation: 关于噪音研究最重要的发现可能是，噪音的可预测性比其大小更为重要。我们更容易"忽略"持续的背景噪音，即使它很大，而不是在有意外噪音干扰的情况下工作。

target_sen.target_sen: We are much more able to 'tune out' chronic background noise, even if it is quite loud, than to work under circumstances with unexpected intrusions of noise.

questions.analysis: 题干中提到所有组都暴露在某种噪音环境中。通过文章中的描述，我们可以找到对应的句子：“others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise)”。这句话明确指出所有组接触到的噪音总量是相同的，因此正确答案是 "the same amount of"。

central_sentences.sentence: For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
central_sentences.translation: 对于一些受试者，噪音爆发的间隔正好是一分钟（可预测的噪音）；其他人总体上听到了相同数量的噪音，但爆发发生在随机间隔（不可预测的噪音）。

target_sen.target_sen: In the Glass and Singer study, in which subjects were exposed to bursts of noise as they worked on a task, some subjects heard loud bursts and others heard soft bursts.

For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.
But the different noise conditions had quite different after-effects when the subjects were required to proofread written material under conditions of no noise.
In the Glass and Singer study, in which subjects were exposed to bursts of noise as they worked on a task, some subjects heard loud bursts and others heard soft bursts.

For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.

As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).

questions.analysis: 在题干中，提到“predictable noise group”和“unpredictable noise group”，并询问它们在任务中的表现。文章中明确指出“all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment”，这表明两个组的表现是相同的。因此，正确答案是“performed at about the same level as”。

central_sentences.sentence: Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.
central_sentences.translation: 受试者报告说可预测和不可预测的噪音同样令人烦恼，在实验的噪音部分，所有受试者的表现水平大致相同。

target_sen.target_sen: For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.

As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.

As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

questions.analysis: 题干中提到的“unpredictable noise”和“predictable noise”在原文中都有明确对应。原文中提到“unpredictable noise produced more errors”，即“不可预测的噪音产生了更多的错误”，这与题干中的“【 】”部分直接对应。因此，正确答案是“I: made more mistakes than”。

central_sentences.sentence: As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise;
central_sentences.translation: 如表1所示，不可预测的噪音在后来的校对任务中产生了比可预测噪音更多的错误；

target_sen.target_sen: For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.

As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.

As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

questions.analysis: 根据文章内容，unpredictable noise 组在校对任务中犯了更多的错误，这与题干中的描述完全一致。因此，正确答案是 I: made more mistakes than。

central_sentences.sentence: As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.
central_sentences.translation: 如表1所示，不可预测的噪音在后续的校对任务中产生了比可预测噪音更多的错误；而且，轻柔的不可预测噪音在这项任务中实际上比响亮的可预测噪音产生了稍微更多的错误。

target_sen.target_sen: For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.

As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

For some subjects, the bursts were spaced exactly one minute apart (predictable noise); others heard the same amount of noise overall, but the bursts occurred at random intervals (unpredictable noise).
Subjects reported finding the predictable and unpredictable noise equally annoying, and all subjects performed at about the same level during the noise portion of the experiment.

As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

groups.title: List of Researchers
groups.desc:
Questions 35-40

Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: Subjects exposed to noise find it difficult at first to concentrate on problem-solving tasks.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“at first”和“difficult”分别对应原文中的“at first”和“disruptive”，而“problem-solving tasks”对应“work out problems”。因此，Glass和Singer的研究描述与题干最为匹配。

central_sentences.sentence: The noise was quite disruptive at first, but after about four minutes the subjects were doing just as well on their tasks as control subjects who were not exposed to noise.
central_sentences.translation: 起初，噪音相当具有干扰性，但大约四分钟后，受试者在任务上的表现就和未暴露于噪音中的对照组受试者一样好了。

target_sen.target_sen: The noise was quite disruptive at first, but after about four minutes the subjects were doing just as well on their tasks as control subjects who were not exposed to noise.
The noise was quite disruptive at first, but after about four minutes the subjects were doing just as well on their tasks as control subjects who were not exposed to noise.

questions.title: Long-term exposure to noise can produce changes in behaviour which can still be observed a year later.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D. Cohen et al.。题干中提到的“长期噪音暴露导致行为变化，并且这些变化在一年后仍然可以观察到”在文章中有明确对应。Cohen et al. 的研究比较了在嘈杂环境和安静环境中上学的学生，并指出这些影响在一年后依然存在。其他选项的研究都没有涉及长期噪音暴露的长期影响。

central_sentences.sentence: A follow-up study showed that children who were moved to less noisy classrooms still showed greater distractibility one year later than students who had always been in the quiet schools (Cohen et al, 1981).
central_sentences.translation: 一项后续研究表明，被转移到较安静教室的儿童在一年后仍然比一直在安静学校就读的学生更容易分心（Cohen等人，1981年）。

syn_ary.synonym: living day after day with chronic noise

target_sen.target_sen: But the major worry about noisy environments is that living day after day with chronic noise may produce serious, lasting effects.

syn_ary.synonym: higher blood pressure and were more easily distracted

target_sen.target_sen: It was found that children from the noisy schools had higher blood pressure and were more easily distracted than those who attended the quiet schools.

A follow-up study showed that children who were moved to less noisy classrooms still showed greater distractibility one year later than students who had always been in the quiet schools (Cohen et al, 1981).

questions.title: The problems associated with exposure to noise do not arise if the subject knows they can make it stop.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "knows they can make it stop" 和 "problems do not arise" 对应文章中的 "knows that he or she can control the noise" 和 "eliminate both its negative effects at the time and its after-effects"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确认题干所描述的研究是由 Glass 和 Singer 进行的。因此，正确答案是 A: Glass and Singer。

central_sentences.sentence: If the individual knows that he or she can control the noise, this seems to eliminate both its negative effects at the time and its after-effects. This is true even if the individual never actually exercises his or her option to turn the noise off (Glass and Singer, 1972). Just the knowledge that one has control is sufficient.
central_sentences.translation: 如果个人知道他们可以控制噪音，这似乎能消除噪音当时的负面影响和后续影响。即使个人从未真正行使关闭噪音的选择权，这也是成立的（Glass和Singer，1972）。仅仅知道自己能控制就足够了。

syn_ary.synonym: knows that he or she can control the noise

target_sen.target_sen: If the individual knows that he or she can control the noise, this seems to eliminate both its negative effects at the time and its after-effects.

locating_words.locating_word: knows they can make it stop

syn_ary.synonym: eliminate both its negative effects at the time and its after-effects

target_sen.target_sen: If the individual knows that he or she can control the noise, this seems to eliminate both its negative effects at the time and its after-effects.

locating_words.locating_word: problems associated with exposure to noise do not arise

questions.title: Exposure to high-pitched noise results in more errors than exposure to low-pitched noise.
questions.analysis: 题干中提到的“high-pitched noise”和“low-pitched noise”在文章中并没有具体提到。文章讨论的是噪声的音量（loud or soft）和可预测性（predictable or unpredictable），而不是噪声的频率。因此，正确答案是E: None of the above，因为文章中没有提到题干中的具体内容。

central_sentences.sentence: one
central_sentences.translation: 文章中没有直接对应的答案句，因为文章并未讨论高音或低音噪声对错误率的影响。

target_sen.target_sen: As shown in Table 1 the unpredictable noise produced more errors in the later proofreading task than predictable noise; and soft, unpredictable noise actually produced slightly more errors on this task than the loud, predictable noise.

questions.title: Subjects find it difficult to perform three tasks at the same time when exposed to noise.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "perform three tasks at the same time" 对应文章中的 "monitor three dials at a time"，而 "exposed to noise" 对应 "high noise levels"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确认题干所描述的研究是由 Broadbent 进行的。因此，正确答案是 B. Broadbent。

central_sentences.sentence: For example, high noise levels interfered with the performance of subjects who were required to monitor three dials at a time, a task not unlike that of an aeroplane pilot or an air-traffic controller (Broadbent, 1957).
central_sentences.translation: 例如，高噪音水平干扰了被要求同时监控三个刻度盘的受试者的表现，这项任务类似于飞机飞行员或空中交通管制员的工作（Broadbent，1957）。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, high noise levels interfered with the performance of subjects who were required to monitor three dials at a time, a task not unlike that of an aeroplane pilot or an air-traffic controller (Broadbent, 1957).

For example, high noise levels interfered with the performance of subjects who were required to monitor three dials at a time, a task not unlike that of an aeroplane pilot or an air-traffic controller (Broadbent, 1957).

questions.title: Noise affects a subject's capacity to repeat numbers while carrying out another task.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Noise affects a subject's capacity to repeat numbers while carrying out another task" 对应文章中的 "noise ... did interfere with the subject's ability to repeat numbers while tracking"。这表明噪音干扰了受试者在执行另一项任务（追踪）时重复数字的能力。根据文章，这项研究是由 Finkelman 和 Glass 进行的，因此正确答案是 C。

central_sentences.sentence: Similarly, noise did not affect a subject's ability to track a moving line with a steering wheel, but it did interfere with the subject's ability to repeat numbers while tracking (Finkelman and Glass, 1970).
central_sentences.translation: 同样地，噪音并不影响受试者用方向盘追踪移动线条的能力，但确实干扰了受试者在追踪的同时重复数字的能力（Finkelman和Glass，1970）。

target_sen.target_sen: Similarly, noise did not affect a subject's ability to track a moving line with a steering wheel, but it did interfere with the subject's ability to repeat numbers while tracking (Finkelman and Glass, 1970).

Similarly, noise did not affect a subject's ability to track a moving line with a steering wheel, but it did interfere with the subject's ability to repeat numbers while tracking (Finkelman and Glass, 1970).
